features.footer: You can use these pages to learn more about features, provided by Telegram Mini Apps and other useful projects

application.title: Browser Application
application.subtitle: Manifest, storage, service worker and browser capability diagnostics

auth.title: Auth Foundation
auth.subtitle: Telegram initData verification, signed session cookie and local preview login

profile.title: Protected Profile
profile.subtitle: Auth-required user profile route and session-backed profile API

premium.title: Premium Wallet Gate
premium.subtitle: A monetization-style route unlocked only by a verified TON wallet session

platform.title: Telegram Platform
platform.subtitle: Main Button, haptics, swipe navigation, skeleton loading and app config

launchData.footer: These pages help developer to learn more about current launch information

initData.title: Init Data
initData.subtitle: User data, chat information, technical data

launchParams.title: Launch Parameters
launchParams.subtitle: Platform identifier, Mini Apps version, etc.

locale.footer: You can select a different language from the dropdown menu.

missing.description: Application was launched with missing init data

empty.title: No theme parameters available
empty.description: Theme parameters are not available in the current environment

disconnected.description: To display the data related to the TON Connect, it is required to connect your wallet

actions.linkWallet: Link connected wallet to current session
actions.relinkWallet: Refresh linked wallet in current session
actions.unlinkWallet: Clear linked wallet from current session

messages.tonProofSuccess: TON wallet verified and promoted into an authenticated wallet-first session.
messages.tonProofError: Unable to verify TON wallet ownership for the current proof payload.
messages.tonProofRejected: The connected wallet did not return a successful ton_proof item.
messages.tonProofMissingPublicKey: The connected wallet did not provide a public key required for local proof verification.
messages.linkSuccess: Connected wallet linked to the current authenticated session.
messages.linkError: Unable to link the connected wallet to the current session.
messages.unlinkSuccess: Linked wallet removed from the current authenticated session.
messages.unlinkError: Unable to clear the linked wallet from the current session.

linking.footer: Linking stores the current wallet identity inside the signed auth session cookie. Full wallet-first auth now uses ton_proof verification and grants premium wallet access.
linking.signInPrompt: Sign in first if you want to link the currently connected wallet to your authenticated session.
linking.walletFirstActive: Wallet-first TON auth session is active.

loading.description: Reading browser capabilities and storage state.

intro.description: Use these actions to expose real Manifest, Service Worker, Storage, Cache Storage and permission state in DevTools > Application.

messages.serviceWorkerRegistered: Service worker registered. Reload once if you want it to control the current page.
messages.demoDataSeeded: Demo entries were written into storage APIs for easier inspection in DevTools.
messages.backgroundSyncRegistered: Background sync was registered on the current service worker.
messages.unsupported: This browser does not expose the requested capability in the current environment.
messages.error: Unable to complete the action in the current browser environment.

application.footer: The manifest link and display mode help verify installability and current shell mode.

serviceWorker.footer: Register the worker to make the Service Workers and Background Sync panels useful.

storage.footer: Use the seed action to populate Local Storage, Session Storage, IndexedDB, Cookies and Cache Storage.

capabilities.footer: These values come from runtime feature detection in the current browser.

browserManaged.footer: Some Application-panel entries depend on browser internals and cannot be directly controlled by app code.

intro.description: This screen demonstrates Phase 1 frontend platform capabilities: Zustand for UI orchestration, TanStack Query for server state, Main Button, haptics, swipe navigation, skeleton loading and app-level feature flags.

messages.idleState: Store is initialized and waiting for interaction.
messages.mainButton: Telegram Main Button click handler executed successfully.
messages.skeleton: Skeleton loading is shown for a short simulated request.
messages.verticalSwipeEnabled: Vertical swipe-to-close is enabled for the current screen.
messages.verticalSwipeDisabled: Vertical swipe-to-close is disabled for the current screen.
messages.unsupported: The current Telegram/browser environment does not expose this capability.

runtime.footer: App config values and Telegram launch parameters are centralized here to make environment handling explicit.

state.footer: This section is driven by a feature-local Zustand store and demonstrates the recommended client-state pattern for app UI orchestration.

serverState.footer: This section is driven by TanStack Query and demonstrates the recommended server-state and cache invalidation pattern.

telegramUx.footer: These rows show the current Main Button, haptics and swipe-navigation state driven by reusable hooks.

links.footer: Use these routes to inspect offline/application diagnostics and wallet integration.

intro.description: This screen demonstrates server-side Telegram initData verification, signed session cookies, session status fetching, logout, and local preview login outside Telegram.

messages.telegramError: Unable to create a Telegram session in the current environment.
messages.devError: Local preview login is not available in the current server configuration.
messages.logoutError: Unable to clear the current session.

capabilities.footer: These values show whether Telegram auth verification and fallback preview login are available on the current server.

session.footer: Signed session cookies are HttpOnly and read through a dedicated server route.

links.footer: Use the init data and platform screens to inspect the raw Telegram context and frontend platform baseline.

intro.unlockedDescription: This premium route is available because the current session was created by a verified TON wallet using ton_proof.

reasons.disabled: Monetization features are currently disabled by app config.
reasons.anonymous: Requires a verified TON wallet session. Connect a wallet and complete ton_proof verification to unlock premium access.
reasons.wallet_linked_only: Linking a wallet to a Telegram/dev session is not enough. Re-authenticate through TON Connect to unlock the premium route.
reasons.authenticated_non_wallet: This session is authenticated, but it was not issued from a verified TON wallet. Use TON Connect to unlock premium access.

status.footer: This entitlement is granted by the verified wallet-first auth flow and can be used as a basis for future paid or gated experiences.

content.body: You now have access to this wallet-gated premium preview. The next increment can replace this demo entitlement with subscription, NFT ownership, or payment-backed access control.

intro.description: This route is server-protected: anonymous users are redirected to /auth, while authenticated users can read their normalized profile via a protected API route.

messages.loadError: Unable to load the authenticated profile. Refresh the session or sign in again.

identity.footer: These values are normalized from the authenticated session and safe to use for UI display.

wallet.footer: These values reflect the TON wallet identity currently linked into the signed auth session.

session.footer: Session timestamps come from the signed cookie payload managed by the auth server layer.

links.footer: Use the auth screen to refresh the session and the platform screen to inspect client/runtime capabilities.
